title: Text Summarizer
problem: Automate text summarization for blog posts or articles to quickly understand their main points.
solution: Utilize the LangChain library to create a text summarization model that summarizes long articles or blog posts, extracting the most important information. The model will leverage the PyPDF2 library to parse and understand the structure of the PDF document.
architecture: Step 1: Collect and preprocess text data from PDF documents using PyPDF2.
Step 2: Use LangChain to create a text summarization model.
Step 3: Train the model on a dataset of PDF articles.
Step 4: Deploy the model using FastAPI for user interaction.
Step 5: Integrate the model with Streamlit for a user-friendly interface.
Step 6: Optimize the model's performance using PyPDF2's built-in functionality.
Step 7: Test and refine the model's output for accurate summarization.

day_plan.Day 1: Collect and preprocess text data from PDF documents.
day_plan.Day 2: Use LangChain to create a text summarization model.
day_plan.Day 3: Train the model on a dataset of PDF articles.
day_plan.Day 7: Test and refine the model's output.

why_impressive: The ability to summarize long articles or blog posts quickly and accurately will make this project stand out for internship or portfolio purposes.
github_tips: Use meaningful commit messages and organize the project structure to ensure easy navigation and understanding of the codebase.

title: House Price Predictor
problem: Predict the price of a house based on its features, helping real estate agents and clients make informed decisions.
solution: This project uses a simple linear regression model trained on a dataset of house prices and features, allowing the user to input house characteristics and receive a predicted price. The model is deployed using Streamlit, enabling easy interaction and visualization.
architecture: Step 1: Collect and preprocess a house price dataset.
Step 2: Split the data into training and testing sets.
Step 3: Train a linear regression model on the training data.
Step 4: Evaluate the model's performance on the test data.

day_plan.Day 1: Collect and preprocess the house price dataset.
day_plan.Day 2: Split the data and train the linear regression model.
day_plan.Day 3: Evaluate the model's performance and deploy it using Streamlit.

why_impressive: This project showcases the student's ability to collect and preprocess data, train a simple machine learning model, and deploy it using Streamlit, highlighting their understanding of the end-to-end data science workflow.
github_tips: The student should include a clear README explaining the dataset, the model, and the code, as well as provide example usage and results.

title: Sentiment Analysis of Customer Reviews
problem: Build a sentiment analysis model that can classify customer reviews as positive, negative, or neutral, and deploy it using a Streamlit dashboard.
solution: This project uses the HuggingFace library to load a pre-trained sentiment analysis model, processes the reviews using Scikit-learn's text preprocessing techniques, and displays the classification results on a Streamlit dashboard. The model is also deployed using the HuggingFace Transformers library.
architecture: Step 1: Load the pre-trained sentiment analysis model using HuggingFace.
Step 2: Process the customer reviews using Scikit-learn's text preprocessing techniques.
Step 3: Classify the reviews using the pre-trained sentiment analysis model.
Step 4: Display the classification results on the Streamlit dashboard.
Step 5: Add a button to allow users to visualize the classification results using Matplotlib.
Step 6: Deploy the model using HuggingFace Transformers.
Step 7: Test the model with sample reviews and deploy it on a cloud platform.

day_plan.Day 1: Load the pre-trained sentiment analysis model using HuggingFace.
day_plan.Day 2: Process the customer reviews using Scikit-learn's text preprocessing techniques.
day_plan.Day 3: Classify the reviews using the pre-trained sentiment analysis model.
day_plan.Day 4: Display the classification results on the Streamlit dashboard.
day_plan.Day 5: Add a button to allow users to visualize the classification results using Matplotlib.
day_plan.Day 6: Deploy the model using HuggingFace Transformers.
day_plan.Day 7: Test the model with sample reviews and deploy it on a cloud platform.

why_impressive: This project showcases the student's ability to build a robust sentiment analysis model using a pre-trained model and Scikit-learn's text preprocessing techniques.
github_tips: Use a clear and concise commit message, and provide a detailed README file that explains the project setup and usage.

title: Sentiment Analysis App
problem: Develop a simple Sentiment Analysis application to determine whether a given text is positive, negative or neutral.
solution: The AI/model uses a pre-trained NLP model to analyze the text and predict the sentiment, while the code handles user input and displays the results through a Streamlit app. The model will be trained and evaluated using Scikit-learn's built-in metrics.
architecture: Step 1: Import necessary libraries and set up a Streamlit app
Step 2: Load a pre-trained NLP model using Scikit-learn
Step 3: Create a function to analyze the sentiment of a given text
Step 4: Display the sentiment analysis results through Streamlit

day_plan.Day 1: Set up Streamlit app and import necessary libraries
day_plan.Day 2: Load pre-trained NLP model and create sentiment analysis function
day_plan.Day 3: Implement user input and result display through Streamlit
day_plan.Day 4: Train and evaluate the model using Scikit-learn
day_plan.Day 6: Add error handling and edge cases

why_impressive: This project showcases basic NLP and application development skills, making it a great addition to an internship portfolio.
github_tips: Use a clear README with installation and usage instructions, and include a sample text file for testing.

title: Trendy T-Shirts
problem: Analyze popular T-shirt designs to find trending colors, patterns, and styles.
solution: Use ChromaDB to collect and analyze design data, then visualize insights with Matplotlib and Streamlit. Identify top colors, patterns, and styles.
architecture: Step 1: Set up ChromaDB and collect design data
Step 2: Clean and preprocess data
Step 3: Use Matplotlib to create insightful visualizations
Step 4: Deploy with Streamlit

day_plan.Day 3: Use Matplotlib to create insightful visualizations
day_plan.Day 4: Design and build the Streamlit app

github_tips: Use clear and concise commit messages and branch names, and provide a detailed README file.
